title: Ensemble Kalman Filter (EnKF)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Ensemble Kalman Filter (EnKF): EnKF: ensemble-based approximation of Kalman filter covariance; scales to large state dimensions. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.enkf.ensemble_update…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; sampling_error_finite_ensemble, localization_radius_choice set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: K = P_f H ** T (H P_f H ** T + R)^{-1}; P_f estimated from ensemble; analysis: x_a ** i = x_f ** i + K(y ** i - Hx_f ** i)
E.world_state_x: ensemble of state estimates {x_a ** i} representing posterior distribution

W.regime: Existence of the recovered ensemble_state_distribution is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); sampling_error_finite_ensemble dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: spread-skill ratio ~ 1 for well-tuned ensemble; localization prevents sampling error. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |sampling_error_finite_ensemble|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_e * n_state * N_obs) per cycle per iteration
